question_html: Write a paragraph on 'Students and Social Service'. 10
solution_html:
Students and Social Service

Students play a vital role in social service because their energy and idealism can bring positive change to society. They can participate in various community activities such as cleaning streets, awareness campaigns, and charity events to support those in need. During natural disasters, students often volunteer to distribute food, provide first aid, and help affected families rebuild their lives. Social service also helps students develop compassion, teamwork, and leadership skills that textbooks alone cannot teach. Through voluntary work, they learn the value of responsibility and understand the real struggles of different groups in society. It encourages them to become active citizens who care about social justice and national development. Moreover, social service reduces selfishness by teaching students to think beyond their personal interests. Schools and colleges can organize programs that guide students toward meaningful community work. Such participation builds confidence and strengthens their moral character. Ultimately, students engaged in social service become valuable contributors to the nation’s future.

Marks Distribution:

Highest Marks --------------------------------------------------------------------09 Marks

(A) For Title (Students and Social Service) ------------------------------------01 Mark

(B) Topic Sentence (Introduction) ---------------------------------------------01 Mark

Students play a vital role in social service because their energy and idealism can bring positive change to society.

C) Context Explanation (Students and Social Service) ( Any 6 Points) ---------------------06 Marks
‎• They engage in community activities, including street cleaning, awareness campaigns, and charity events.
‎• During natural disasters, students volunteer for food distribution, first aid, and assisting affected families.
‎• Participation in social service fosters compassion, teamwork, and leadership skills beyond academic learning.
‎• Voluntary work instills a sense of responsibility and awareness of diverse societal struggles.
‎• It encourages students to become active citizens committed to social justice and national development.
‎• Involvement in social service mitigates selfishness, promoting a focus on collective interests.
‎• Educational institutions can facilitate programs to guide students towards meaningful community engagement.
‎• Such involvement enhances confidence and strengthens moral character.

D) Conclusion --------------------------------------------------------------------------01 Mark
‎Ultimately, students engaged in social service become valuable contributors to the nation’s future.

Instructions:

1. For every 3 spelling/grammatical mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.

2. Students must write within a para. If a student writes more than one para, half of the deserving marks must be deducted. (Comment: Write within single/one para).

3. For writing only the title of the paragraph bears no mark.

4. For writing only the title of the paragraph bears no mark. (comment: Write the Title/ Students and Social Service)

question_html:
Make meaningful sentences with each of the following words: 5

Desperately, Fluctuate, Optimistic, Impediment, Benevolent
solution_html:
Desperately (Adverb) : They desperately tried to save the injured man before the ambulance finally arrived.

Fluctuate (Verb) : Prices of essential goods often fluctuate during festival seasons due to increased market demand.

Optimistic (Adjective) : We felt optimistic after hearing the encouraging news about the project’s progress.

Impediment (Noun): Their boycott of the talks constitutes a serious impediment to peace negotiations.

Benevolent (Adjective) : The benevolent woman donated winter clothes to the poor without expecting anything in return.

Instructions:

(i) Any correct and logically meaningful sentence will be accepted. (Sentences with weak meaning will not be allowed.)

(ii) For any grammatical or structural mistake, full mark for this item must be deducted. Examiners must underline and correct the mistake(s) in the scripts.

(iii) For 02 spelling mistakes, 01 mark will be deducted, for 01 spelling mistake, 0.5 marks will be deducted from that very sentence.
‎(iv) For changing the part of speech of the given word by adding a suffix or prefix, the full marks have to be deducted from the very number. But students can use any form if the given word is a verb (depending on the subject-verb agreement, tense, person, number, etc.).
‎Comment: Use the given word without changing its parts of speech.

question_html:
The following is the beginning of a story. Complete it in your own words. 5

One day I was going to college to take my final exam at 10 am. At 9 when I was 20 minutes away from my college, suddenly a sick old man fell down……
solution_html:
A Timely Act of Kindness

One day, I was going to college to take my final exam at 10 am. At 9, when I was 20 minutes away from my college, suddenly a sick old man fell down in front of me. He looked distressed and unable to get up.

Without hesitation, I rushed to his side and checked his condition. He was clutching his chest and gasping for breath. I immediately called for an ambulance while trying to keep him calm. Nearby, I asked a passerby to help me get him to a nearby bench.

The ambulance arrived within minutes, and the paramedics took over. I gave them my contact details and informed them of the old man's condition. After ensuring he was in safe hands, I hurried to my college. Although I arrived just in time for the exam, the incident made me realize the importance of compassion and the impact of timely assistance.

Marks Distribution

Highest mark---------------05 Marks

(i)Title (Any logical one) -------(01 Mark has to be deducted for not writing the title) “A Timely Act of Kindness”

(ii) Story (minimum new 5 sentences) 1×5=05 Marks

{If a student adds or writes at least 5-7 new sentences using the given part and completes the story with a proper description without any grammatical or spelling mistakes, he/she will get full marks}.

Instructions:

(i) If a student doesn’t write the given part, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the given part first).

(ii) Students can write the story in any logical way (sample answer isn’t fixed).

(iii) The given part tells us that the story is the incidents of the Past. So, it is normal that the student will develop the story using Past Tense but it can be described in Present Tense and future Tense also by using direct speech.

(iv) If a student doesn’t write the Title, 01 mark has to be deducted (comment: write the Title/ A Timely

Act of Kindness)

question_html:
(i) Read the text below and fill in the gaps with the clues given in the box. There are more words than necessary. Make any grammatical changes (if necessary). 5

Hospitality has long been a (a) _____ of our culture in Bangladesh. Although many other things are (b) _____. Now, people are still quite polite and friendly (c) _____ foreigners. They like speaking to foreigners (d) _____ if they have not been introduced. It is quite common for Bangladesh to (e) _____ personal questions about family, jobs and income which visitors (f) _____ overseas might find awkward. However, it should be understood that (g) _____ harm is meant by such (h) _____. On the other (i) _____ they express a genuine interest in the foreigner’s state of affairs. The whole country has a fairly (j) _____ communication network now.

(ii) Fill in the gaps with suitable words: 5

Artificial intelligence has the (a)________ to revolutionise the way we learn and teach. As a tool in the classroom, AI can (b)______ students with personalised learning experiences, automate (c)________ tasks and provide (d)________ feedback. (e)________, it also has its limitations.
solution_html:
(i)

(a) part

(b) changing

(c) towards

(d) even

(e) ask

(f) from

(g) no

(h) questions

(i) hand

(j) good

Instructions:

Any mistake bears no mark. However, mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

(ii)

(a) potential

(b) provide

(c) repetitive

(d) instant

(e) However

Instructions:

Any mistake bears no mark. However, mistakes regarding capital and small letters can be overlooked.

question_html:
Translate the following paragraph into English: 5

আত্মনির্ভরশীলতা বলতে বোঝায় নিজের ওপর নির্ভরশীলতা। নিজেকে সাহায্য করাই সর্বোৎকৃষ্ট সাহায্য। সৃষ্টিকর্তা তাঁদের সাহায্য করেন যারা নিজেদের সাহায্য করেন। তাই আত্মনির্ভরশীল হওয়ার জন্য প্রত্যেককে অবশ্যই তার নিজের কর্মদক্ষতার ওপর নির্ভর করতে হবে। একজন আত্মনির্ভরশীল ব্যক্তির তার নিজের কর্মদক্ষতার ওপর আত্মবিশ্বাস রয়েছে।
solution_html:
অনুবাদ: Self-reliance means being dependent on one’s own self. Self-help is the best help. God helps those who help themselves. So, everyone must depend on his/her own working abilities to be self-reliant. A self-reliant man has confidence in his own abilities.

Instructions:
‎* For accurate translation using grammar, punctuation, and sentence structure, students will get full marks. [05 marks for translating 05 sentences correctly]

* For every 03 grammatical/spelling errors 01 mark has to be deducted.

question_html: Explain the proverb: “Practice makes a man perfect.” 5
solution_html:
The proverb “Practice makes a man perfect” means that the more we do something, the better we become at it. No one becomes skilled or successful overnight. It takes time, effort, and regular practice to improve. Whether it’s playing a sport, learning something, reading, writing, or doing any other task, repeating it again and again helps us learn from our mistakes and get better each time. This saying encourages us not to give up if we are not good at something in the beginning. With patience and continuous practice, we can become perfect or at least very good at what we do.

Mark Distribution:

A) Main theme of the proverb-----------------01 mark
‎• The proverb “Practice makes a man perfect” emphasizes the idea that achieving success, improvement, or rewards requires hard work, effort, and perseverance.

B) Context of The Proverb (Practice makes a man perfect) (03 points) -----03 marks

• Skill development requires time, effort, and consistent practice; success is not instantaneous.
‎• Engaging in various activities, such as sports, reading, or writing, enables us to learn from mistakes and improve.
‎• The saying serves as motivation to persist despite initial difficulties.

C) Concluding Sentence……….01 Mark

• Ultimately, with patience and continuous practice, we can become perfect or at least very good at what we do.

Instructions:

For every 03 spelling mistakes, 01 mark has to be deducted.
